summary: Current dev/skill dogfood and packet tests prove that the dev/skill preset can evaluate multi-turn agent episodes when fixtures provide turns, and that `eval evaluate` remains the deterministic packet boundary for already-observed skill trigger and execution quality.

evidenceScope.targetSurface: dev/skill episode turns and skill evaluation packet boundary
evidenceScope.claimBoundary: This bundle supports only multi-turn dev/skill episode handling and the packet-level `eval evaluate` skill summary boundary. It does not prove every possible skill prompt, Claude parity for the new episode, or app/product behavior.
notClaimed: Every skill fixture with turns is semantically good
Claude runtime parity for the canonical spec curation episode
Fixture runtime is valid for episode adapters without fixture results
HITL review or claim review prepare-input execution

observed.summary: Execution consensus reached passed in 1/1 run(s). Audit passed: 0 finding(s).

protectedBehaviors: skill evaluation summaries normalize trigger and execution quality
fixture-backed skill test results materialize `cautilus.skill_evaluation_inputs.v1` observed packets
audit-backed episode turns round-trip through skill case suite normalization
canonical spec curation audit episodes are accepted
`eval evaluate` consumes observed packets without launching adapter runners

relatedEvidence.reason: Provides the Codex-backed dev/skill episode run whose fixture uses multi-turn `turns`.

decision.reason: The current checked-in dev/skill episode fixture with `turns` passed through eval-test, and the deterministic skill evaluation tests prove `eval evaluate` remains the packet-level boundary for skill trigger and execution quality.
